---
title: "Open Compilations"
description: "A static, OneNote-style reference notebook. Each topic ships with Obsidian-ready Markdown you can copy or download — individually or as a ZIP."
toc: true
toc-location: left
toc-title: "Pages"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple" icon=false}
### 📓 How this page works

Each topic below is **read-only static reference material**, organised like a OneNote notebook:

> **High-level area → sub-page → topic notes**

Every topic has two views — the rendered note you read, and a copyable **Obsidian markdown source** complete with YAML frontmatter, `#tags`, and `[[wikilinks]]` you can paste straight into a vault.

[📥 Download all notes as a ZIP](files/obsidian/open-compilations.zip){.btn .btn-primary role="button"}
&nbsp;
[🗂 Map of content (`_index.md`)](files/obsidian/_index.md){.btn .btn-outline-primary role="button"}
:::

# Science → Physics

::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills}

## ⚡ Electromagnetic Radiation

The whole spectrum, ordered low energy → high energy → wave reception. Each band has its own dominant physics for converting photons into a signal.

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Visible light

> **Mechanism:** photoelectric & photovoltaic effect

| Detector | What it is |
|---|---|
| **Photodiode** | Silicon p-n junction; absorbs a visible photon, generates an electron–hole pair, drives a reverse current. The cheapest workhorse for *is there light*. |
| **CCD** | Charge-coupled device array; each pixel collects photo-generated charge then shuttles it serially to a single readout amplifier. Low noise, slow. |
| **CMOS** | Active-pixel sensor; each pixel has its own amplifier and addressable readout. Fast, cheap, dominates phone and consumer cameras. |
| **Photoresistor (LDR)** | Cadmium-sulfide film; resistance drops with light intensity. Slow, analog, used in night lights and dawn/dusk switches. |
| **Phototransistor** | BJT or FET with light-controlled base / gate; gives gain compared to a plain photodiode but is slower. |
| **PMT** | Photomultiplier tube; photocathode emits electrons that cascade through dynodes for 10⁶–10⁸ gain. Single-photon counting in vacuum. |

[📄 Download `visible-light.md`](files/obsidian/visible-light.md){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary role="button"}

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## 📝 Obsidian markdown source — click to expand

````markdown
---
tags:
  - science/physics
  - electromagnetic-radiation
  - visible-light
  - sensors
created: 2026-05-16
breadcrumb: "Science > Physics > Electromagnetic radiation > Visible light"
---

# Visible light

> **Mechanism:** photoelectric & photovoltaic effect

## Detectors

- **Photodiode** — Silicon p-n junction; absorbs a visible photon, generates an electron-hole pair, drives a reverse current. The cheapest workhorse for "is there light."
- **CCD** — Charge-coupled device array; each pixel collects photo-generated charge, then shuttles it serially to a single readout amplifier. Low noise, slow.
- **CMOS** — Active-pixel sensor; each pixel has its own amplifier and addressable readout. Fast, cheap, dominates phone and consumer cameras.
- **Photoresistor (LDR)** — Cadmium-sulfide film; resistance drops with light intensity. Slow, analog, used in night lights and dawn/dusk switches.
- **Phototransistor** — BJT or FET with light-controlled base or gate; provides gain compared to a plain photodiode but is slower.
- **PMT** — Photomultiplier tube; photocathode emits electrons that cascade through dynodes for 10^6–10^8 gain. Single-photon counting in vacuum.

## Related

- [[infrared]]
- [[ultraviolet]]
````
:::

### Infrared

> **Mechanism:** thermal & near-IR photon detection

| Detector | What it is |
|---|---|
| **Thermopile** | Many thermocouples in series; IR heats a hot junction, voltage measures the temperature difference. Inside non-contact thermometers. |
| **Pyroelectric (PIR)** | Polarisation in a crystal shifts with temperature change; output is AC-coupled. The motion sensor in burglar alarms. |
| **Bolometer** | Temperature-dependent resistor; absorbs IR, resistance changes. The original thermal-imaging primitive. |
| **InGaAs photodiode** | Short-wave-IR (0.9–1.7 µm) semiconductor; standard for fibre-optic links and SWIR cameras. |
| **Microbolometer array** | Uncooled thermal-imaging chip; each pixel is a tiny bolometer. The detector behind every modern thermal camera. |

[📄 Download `infrared.md`](files/obsidian/infrared.md){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary role="button"}

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## 📝 Obsidian markdown source — click to expand

````markdown
---
tags:
  - science/physics
  - electromagnetic-radiation
  - infrared
  - sensors
created: 2026-05-16
breadcrumb: "Science > Physics > Electromagnetic radiation > Infrared"
---

# Infrared

> **Mechanism:** thermal & near-IR photon detection

## Detectors

- **Thermopile** — Many thermocouples in series; IR heats a hot junction, voltage measures the temperature difference. The detector inside non-contact thermometers.
- **Pyroelectric (PIR)** — Polarisation in a crystal shifts with temperature change; output is AC-coupled. The motion sensor in burglar alarms and automatic lights.
- **Bolometer** — Temperature-dependent resistor (typically a thin film); absorbs IR, resistance changes. The original thermal-imaging primitive.
- **InGaAs photodiode** — Short-wave-IR (0.9–1.7 µm) semiconductor; standard for fibre-optic links and SWIR cameras.
- **Microbolometer array** — Uncooled thermal-imaging chip; each pixel is a tiny bolometer. The detector behind every modern thermal camera.

## Related

- [[visible-light]]
- [[ultraviolet]]
````
:::

### Ultraviolet

> **Mechanism:** high-energy photon detection

| Detector | What it is |
|---|---|
| **SiC photodiode** | Silicon carbide; wide-bandgap, solar-blind (rejects visible). Used for flame detection. |
| **GaN photodiode** | Gallium nitride; deep-UV (200–365 nm) semiconductor detector. |
| **UV PMT** | PMT with a bialkali or solar-blind photocathode; single-photon UV counting. |
| **Phototube** | Simple vacuum diode with a photocathode (no dynodes). Historical UV detector. |

[📄 Download `ultraviolet.md`](files/obsidian/ultraviolet.md){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary role="button"}

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## 📝 Obsidian markdown source — click to expand

````markdown
---
tags:
  - science/physics
  - electromagnetic-radiation
  - ultraviolet
  - sensors
created: 2026-05-16
breadcrumb: "Science > Physics > Electromagnetic radiation > Ultraviolet"
---

# Ultraviolet

> **Mechanism:** high-energy photon detection

## Detectors

- **SiC photodiode** — Silicon carbide; wide-bandgap, solar-blind (rejects visible). Used for flame detection.
- **GaN photodiode** — Gallium nitride; deep-UV (200–365 nm) semiconductor detector.
- **UV PMT** — PMT with a bialkali or solar-blind photocathode; single-photon UV counting.
- **Phototube** — Simple vacuum diode with a photocathode (no dynodes). Historical UV detector.

## Related

- [[visible-light]]
- [[x-ray-gamma]]
````
:::

### X-ray & gamma ray

> **Mechanism:** ionizing photon interaction

| Detector | What it is |
|---|---|
| **Scintillator + PMT** | A high-Z crystal (NaI, BGO, LYSO) converts γ → visible; a PMT reads. Backbone of nuclear medicine and security imaging. |
| **HPGe detector** | High-purity germanium semiconductor; gold standard for γ-ray spectroscopy (~0.2 % energy resolution at 1 MeV). LN₂-cooled. |
| **CdTe / CZT** | Cadmium telluride or cadmium-zinc-telluride; room-temperature γ semiconductor. Compact imaging. |
| **Geiger–Müller tube** | Gas-filled tube; an ionising photon triggers an avalanche that's counted. No energy information — just counts. |
| **Image intensifier** | Photocathode → MCP → phosphor screen. Converts low-light, UV, or X-ray into a viewable image. Night vision. |

[📄 Download `x-ray-gamma.md`](files/obsidian/x-ray-gamma.md){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary role="button"}

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## 📝 Obsidian markdown source — click to expand

````markdown
---
tags:
  - science/physics
  - electromagnetic-radiation
  - x-ray
  - gamma-ray
  - ionizing-radiation
  - sensors
created: 2026-05-16
breadcrumb: "Science > Physics > Electromagnetic radiation > X-ray & gamma ray"
---

# X-ray & gamma ray

> **Mechanism:** ionizing photon interaction

## Detectors

- **Scintillator + PMT** — A high-Z crystal (NaI, BGO, LYSO) converts a γ-photon into visible photons; a PMT reads them. Backbone of nuclear medicine and security imaging.
- **HPGe detector** — High-purity germanium semiconductor; the gold standard for γ-ray spectroscopy (~0.2 % energy resolution at 1 MeV). Liquid-nitrogen cooled.
- **CdTe / CZT** — Cadmium telluride or cadmium-zinc-telluride; room-temperature γ semiconductor. Compact imaging without cryogenics.
- **Geiger–Müller tube** — Gas-filled tube; an ionising photon triggers an avalanche that is counted. No energy information — just counts.
- **Image intensifier** — Photocathode → micro-channel plate → phosphor screen. Converts low-light, UV, or X-ray into a viewable image. The optics behind night vision.

## Related

- [[ultraviolet]]
````
:::

### Radio, microwave, THz

> **Mechanism:** electromagnetic wave reception

| Detector | What it is |
|---|---|
| **Antenna + rectifier** | Classical RF reception; the antenna captures the field, a diode rectifier demodulates. |
| **Crystal diode** | Point-contact diode demodulator. The original "cat's-whisker" AM radio detector. |
| **Microwave bolometer** | Thermal RF detector; absorbed power heats a sensitive resistor. |
| **SIS junction** | Superconductor-insulator-superconductor tunnel junction; quantum mixer for mm-wave and submm radio astronomy (ALMA receivers). |
| **HEMT mixer** | High-electron-mobility transistor; low-noise mixing across microwave bands. The front-end of modern radio telescopes. |

[📄 Download `radio-microwave-thz.md`](files/obsidian/radio-microwave-thz.md){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary role="button"}

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## 📝 Obsidian markdown source — click to expand

````markdown
---
tags:
  - science/physics
  - electromagnetic-radiation
  - radio
  - microwave
  - terahertz
  - sensors
created: 2026-05-16
breadcrumb: "Science > Physics > Electromagnetic radiation > Radio, microwave, THz"
---

# Radio, microwave, THz

> **Mechanism:** electromagnetic wave reception

## Detectors

- **Antenna + rectifier** — Classical RF reception; the antenna captures the field, a diode rectifier demodulates.
- **Crystal diode** — Point-contact diode demodulator. The original "cat's-whisker" AM radio detector.
- **Microwave bolometer** — Thermal RF detector; absorbed power heats a sensitive resistor whose change is measured.
- **SIS junction** — Superconductor-insulator-superconductor tunnel junction; a quantum mixer used in mm-wave and submm radio astronomy (ALMA).
- **HEMT mixer** — High-electron-mobility transistor; low-noise mixing across microwave bands. The amplifier in radio telescopes.

## Related

- [[visible-light]]
````
:::

:::

## 🔧 Mechanical — motion, force, acoustic waves

How systems sense their own movement through space. The two big buckets are linear motion (acceleration) and rotation (angular rate).

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Acceleration

> **Mechanism:** Newton's second law on a proof mass

| Detector | What it is |
|---|---|
| **MEMS capacitive** | Silicon proof mass on flexible tethers; displacement changes a capacitance gap. The accelerometer in every phone. |
| **Piezoelectric** | Charge develops across a piezoelectric crystal (quartz, PZT) under stress. AC-coupled — cannot measure constant acceleration. |
| **Piezoresistive** | Bonded or implanted resistor whose resistance changes with strain. DC-capable but temperature sensitive. |
| **Servo / force-balance** | Closed-loop nulling: a feedback coil pushes the proof mass back to rest; the drive current is the output. Highest-accuracy class. |

[📄 Download `acceleration.md`](files/obsidian/acceleration.md){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary role="button"}

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## 📝 Obsidian markdown source — click to expand

````markdown
---
tags:
  - science/physics
  - mechanical
  - acceleration
  - sensors
  - mems
created: 2026-05-16
breadcrumb: "Science > Physics > Mechanical — motion, force, acoustic waves > Acceleration"
---

# Acceleration

> **Mechanism:** Newton's second law on a proof mass

## Detectors

- **MEMS capacitive** — Silicon proof mass on flexible tethers; displacement changes a capacitance gap. The accelerometer in every phone.
- **Piezoelectric** — Charge develops across a piezoelectric crystal (quartz, PZT) under stress. AC-coupled — cannot measure constant acceleration.
- **Piezoresistive** — Bonded or implanted resistor whose resistance changes with strain. DC-capable but temperature sensitive.
- **Servo / force-balance** — Closed-loop nulling: a feedback coil pushes the proof mass back to rest; the drive current is the output. Highest-accuracy class.

## Related

- [[angular-rate]]
````
:::

### Angular rate / rotation

> **Mechanism:** Coriolis & Sagnac effects

| Detector | What it is |
|---|---|
| **MEMS gyroscope** | Vibrating proof mass plus Coriolis effect; the gyro in every phone. |
| **Fibre-optic gyro (FOG)** | Two laser beams travel a long fibre loop in opposite directions; rotation produces a phase shift (Sagnac effect). |
| **Ring laser gyro (RLG)** | Counter-propagating lasers in a closed ring; rotation changes the beat frequency between them. Aircraft-grade gyro. |
| **Spinning-rotor gyro** | A rapidly spinning gimballed rotor preserves orientation. Legacy aerospace inertial reference. |

[📄 Download `angular-rate.md`](files/obsidian/angular-rate.md){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary role="button"}

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## 📝 Obsidian markdown source — click to expand

````markdown
---
tags:
  - science/physics
  - mechanical
  - angular-rate
  - gyroscope
  - sensors
created: 2026-05-16
breadcrumb: "Science > Physics > Mechanical — motion, force, acoustic waves > Angular rate / rotation"
---

# Angular rate / rotation

> **Mechanism:** Coriolis & Sagnac effects

## Detectors

- **MEMS gyroscope** — Vibrating proof mass plus Coriolis effect; the gyro in every phone.
- **Fibre-optic gyro (FOG)** — Two laser beams travel a long fibre loop in opposite directions; rotation produces a phase shift (Sagnac effect).
- **Ring laser gyro (RLG)** — Counter-propagating lasers in a closed ring cavity; rotation changes the beat frequency between them. Aircraft-grade gyro.
- **Spinning-rotor gyro** — A rapidly spinning gimballed rotor preserves orientation. Legacy aerospace inertial reference.

## Related

- [[acceleration]]
````
:::

:::

:::

## Adding your own notes

This page is intentionally append-only — new high-level areas (Chemistry, Biology, Engineering, …) will arrive over time as I compile them. Each new leaf ships:

1. A rendered note here, in the same tabs-and-tables format.
2. A copyable Obsidian markdown source (frontmatter, `#tags`, `[[wikilinks]]`).
3. A `.md` file in `/files/obsidian/` for one-click download.

If there's a specific topic you'd like me to compile next, [open an issue on GitHub](https://github.com/kader-xai).
